agent_reply: The crash was from the SD test calling `AudioManager::init()`, which calls `M5.begin()`. On this stack that brings up M5Unified I2C and conflicts with Arduino’s old I2C driver:
`driver_ng is not allowed to be used with this old driver`

I changed [test_sdcard.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_sdcard.cpp:1) so the smoke test no longer initializes `AudioManager` or M5 at all. It now creates its own two `AudioFifo`s and calls `AudioFileRecorder::init(...)` directly.

I also hardened [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:49) so if RTC time is unavailable, recording filenames use a deterministic fallback timestamp instead of uninitialized date fields.
Build passes. You’ll want to flash this version and the boot loop should be gone.

user_prompt: explain the problem with I2C driver, and where it is
agent_reply: The error is an ESP-IDF 5.x driver-family conflict.
Your firmware is pulling in two incompatible I2C APIs at the same time:
ESP-IDF explicitly aborts if both are used in one firmware image. That is the line in your serial log:
E i2c: CONFLICT! driver_ng is not allowed to be used with this old driver
Where it came from in this case:
- `M5.begin()` enters M5Unified/M5GFX hardware setup, including I2C setup for RTC/power/touch/display-related devices.
- M5Unified’s I2C wrapper includes the legacy driver here:

The other side of the conflict is likely from the newer ESP32 Arduino core / framework libraries in this platform version, where some component has already initialized or linked the new IDF I2C driver. Once `M5.begin()` tries to bring up the legacy side, IDF detects both and aborts very early.
That is why the fix was to avoid `AudioManager::init()` in the SD-card smoke test. The SD test only needs:
It does **not** need M5 screen, RTC, PMIC, speaker, mic, touch, or I2C. So the test now initializes `AudioFileRecorder` directly and skips the whole `M5.begin()` path.

user_prompt: now it's just an abort error
--- Available filters and text transformations: debug, default, direct, esp32_exception_decoder, hexlify, log2file, nocontrol, printable, send_on_enter, time
--- Quit: Ctrl+C | Menu: Ctrl+T | Help: Ctrl+T followed by Ctrl+H
abort() was called at PC 0x401482bf on core 0

E (966) esp_core_dump_elf: Elf write init failed!

E (966) esp_core_dump_elf: Elf write init failed!
E (970) esp_core_dump_common: Core dump write failed with error=-1
and the exception decoder isn't working
agent_reply: The abort decoded to `operator new[]` inside `AudioFifo::AudioFifo(...)`, called from the global `AudioFifo` objects in `src/tests/test_sdcard.cpp`. So the crash was happening before `setup()`, during static initialization.

I made a tight fix in [test_sdcard.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_sdcard.cpp:18): the test FIFOs are now function-local statics, so they construct after `Serial.begin()` inside `test_sdcard()`, not during boot globals. I also added heap prints around FIFO init at [test_sdcard.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_sdcard.cpp:203), and trimmed the FIFO capacity from `8192` to `4096` samples.

The exception decoder is already configured in `platformio.ini`, but it didn’t decode that monitor output. For this trace, manual decode was the useful path; if it still fails in monitor, make sure you’re monitoring the same `m5stack-core2` env immediately after building/flashing so it has the matching `.elf`.

user_prompt: keep the heap prints but remove the new instances of fifos

edit `lib\AudioFifo\AudioFifo.h` so that it needs an explicit call to `begin` in order to actually make the buffer, thus actually resolving this problem instead of leaving that class as a ticking time bomb
change AudioManager to make that call to `.begin` on the fifos
change `test_sdcard` to delegate the initialization of the fifos and the `AudioFileRecorder` to `AudioManager::init()`
agent_reply: Done. I made the FIFO allocation explicit instead of hiding it in static construction.

Changed [AudioFifo.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFifo/AudioFifo.h:37) so the constructor only stores config, and `begin()` does the actual `new (std::nothrow)` buffer allocation. FIFO methods now safely return empty/no-op behavior if someone uses one before `begin()`.

Changed [AudioManager.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:448) so `AudioManager::init()` calls `begin()` on all four manager FIFOs, then initializes `AudioFileRecorder` with the file FIFOs.

Changed [test_sdcard.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_sdcard.cpp:192) so it delegates FIFO + recorder setup to `AudioManager::init()`, keeps the heap prints, and queues test samples through `AudioManager::bluetoothToFileFifo()` / `micToFileFifo()`.
Verified with `pio run -e m5stack-core2`: build succeeds. There is still the existing packed-member warning in `AudioFileRecorder.cpp`, unrelated to this change.

user_prompt: modify `test_sdcard`, instead of stuffing a fixed number of samples, do the following
each chunk is 1/4th the total fifo capacity, stuff the fifo, pump it to file, as fast as possible, for 60 seconds
keep track of how many samples have been submitted to the FIFO, report that statistic every 5 seconds, and also report it as bytes per second in each report
do not modify any other files other than `src\tests\test_sdcard.cpp`
agent_reply: `test_sdcard()` now runs a 60-second throughput test after starting the recording. It uses chunks sized to 1/4 of the effective FIFO capacity, submits chunks to both recording FIFOs as fast as it can, pumps to the file immediately, and reports every 5 seconds:
- interval bytes per second, based on submitted PCM bytes

commit_message: got a physical unit, starting bringup tests

user_prompt: every 30 seconds, chunk_samples doubles, the test ends when chunk_samples is at max_capacity_samples for 30 seconds. Have a print when the switch occurs so we can see which section of log is what chunk_size.
the report should also include the `writeDurationAverageMs` and `writeDurationMaxMs`, and call `resetWriteDurationStats`.
agent_reply: - Stops after the max chunk size has run for 30 seconds.
- Prints a `throughput section` line whenever the chunk size changes.
- Calls `AudioFileRecorder::resetWriteDurationStats()` after each report and at each section switch.
I used the shared FIFO capacity as the max: `min(bt_capacity_samples, mic_capacity_samples)`, so one chunk can fit in either FIFO.

commit_message: clean up some tests a bit

user_prompt: I want to run `void test_sdcard()` to test performance of the recorder with encryption enabled
make sure everything is wired so that it works with encryption actually built and active
agent_reply: Wired `test_sdcard()` so it should actually test encrypted recorder performance now.
- `setup()` now calls `all_init()` before bring-up tests, so NVS/M5/gui basics exist before `Aegis` and SD setup.
- installs a deterministic test master key only if no key already exists,
- forces `AudioFileRecorder::setPurePcmMode(false)` so it does not bypass encryption.
- `AudioFileRecorder` now includes `Aegis` by relative path so PlatformIO library discovery works.
- `BtHostList.h` and `WifiManager.h` now include `conf.h`, so their security-mode structs match their `.cpp` files.
Build passed. I restored the generated `src/version.c` bump afterward.

user_prompt: we are reworking how security works, there are now two keys, filecrypt-key and network-key, I want the filecrypt key to have limited exposure.
first, fill in `kSaltNetwork` with "36 22 5c 86 c1 70 f2 c1 11 d3 d6 df 57 6e 28 93 71 4e 52 6c d8 43 b2 6b df ee 1f 12 c9 a3 e5 b2 "

then extend `Aegis::init` to load two keys into memory, `hasMasterKey` should not be renamed and reflect if both keys are available. But `getMasterKey` and `setMasterKey` and similar getters and setters now need to reflect the names `filecrypt` and `network`
`setTestTempMasterKey` also needs to be split into two and `setTestTempPassword` needs to create both keys
agent_reply: - `Aegis::init()` now loads both `filecrypt_key` and `network_key` from NVS.
- `hasMasterKey()` still exists, and now returns true only when both keys are available.
- `setTestTempPassword()` now derives both keys using separate salts.
- Audio recording encryption now uses only the filecrypt key.
- Web auth/config encryption/cloud upload auth now use the network key.
- Updated `test_sdcard()` to install deterministic test keys for both slots.
Also made `Aegis::init()` open NVS read-only and close the handle after loading; setters open write access only when actually saving keys.
Build passed. I restored `src/version.c` after PlatformIO regenerated it.
- `Aegis::init()` now loads both `filecrypt_key` and `network_key` from NVS.
- `hasMasterKey()` still exists, and now returns true only when both keys are available.
- `setTestTempPassword()` now derives both keys using separate salts.
- Audio recording encryption now uses only the filecrypt key.
- Web auth/config encryption/cloud upload auth now use the network key.
- Updated `test_sdcard()` to install deterministic test keys for both slots.
Also made `Aegis::init()` open NVS read-only and close the handle after loading; setters open write access only when actually saving keys.
Build passed. I restored `src/version.c` after PlatformIO regenerated it.
